title: Friday Sermon 4 October 2002
description:
Allah has prepared for them a severe torment; so be mindful of Allah O ye men of understanding, who have believed.  Allah has indeed sent down to you a Reminder personifying Divine glorification —
  
A Messenger who recites unto you the clear revelations of Allah that he may bring those who believe and act piously out of every darkness into the light.  So whosoever believes in Allah and acts piously will He admit into the Gardens with rivers flowing through to abide therein for ever.  Allah has indeed made excellent provision for them.

('The Holy Quran'.  Al Talaq [The Divorce]: 11, 12).

Ibn Jarir al-Tabari writes: The verses speak of a Reminder in the shape of The Holy Quran being sent down by Allah the Exalted and also a Messenger who recited Signs of Allah from the Reminder and enjoined and guided so that the world would come out of darkness and into Noor or Light.  This Messenger indeed was the Holy Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be on him).

The Promised Messiah (on whom be peace) wrote that when God decreed to send His Messenger the darkness of ignorance and obliviousness covered the earth: 

… God has sent His Book and His Messenger, who recites the Word of Allah to you, so that the believing and righteous ones may be brought out of every darkness into light.
  
Thus, in all these verses, God Almighty has clearly stated that the age in which the Holy Prophet, may peace and blessings of Allah be upon him, was sent and 'The Holy Quran' was revealed was engulfed in the darkness of error and misguidance and there was not a  single nation which had escaped this darkness.

(Hazrat Mirza Ghulam Ahmad.  'Barahin e Ahmadiyya: iv'.  'Ruhani Khazain'.  649.  English: Islam International Publications Ltd, 2016.  61).

That light of high degree that was bestowed on perfect man was not in angels, was not in stars, was not in the moon, was not in the sun, was not in the oceans and the rivers, was not in rubies, emeralds, sapphires, or pearls; in short, it was not in any earthly or heavenly object. It was only in perfect man whose highest and loftiest and most perfect example was our lord and master, the Chief of the Prophets, the Chief of all living ones, Muhammad, the chosen one (peace and blessings of Allah be on him).  That light was bestowed on this man and, according to their ranks, upon all those who bore the same colour to some degree.... This dignity was found in its highest and most perfect form in our lord and master and our guide, the immaculate Prophet, the righteous one, testified to by the righteous, Muhammad, the chosen one (peace and blessings of Allah be on him).

(Ahmad.  'Aina e kamalat e Islam'.  Riyaz e Hind, 1893.  'R. K: v'. 160, 161.  'The Essence of Islam'.  197).

A revelation of the Promised Messiah (on whom be peace) with reference to Noor or Divine Light: Say 'I have received Allah’s Noor (Light), if you are a believer then do not reject it'.

The text https://www.alislam.org/urdu/sermon/FST20021004-UR.pdf
